content.problemStatement:
Chef has a circular array AAA of NNN integers A1,A2‚Ä¶ANA_1, A_2 \ldots A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč‚Ä¶AN‚Äč where ANA_NAN‚Äč and A1A_1A1‚Äč are considered adjacent.

The subarray Ai,jA_{i,j}Ai,j‚Äč defined as an array formed using elements of AAA from ithi^{th}ith index element to jthj^{th}jth index element (both inclusive). Formally, Ai,jA_{i, j}Ai,j‚Äč is defined as:


If i‚Č§ji \leq ji‚Č§j, then the subarray of AAA from the index iii to index jjj, both inclusive.
If i>ji > ji>j, then it denotes the subarray of AAA from index iii to index NNN, followed by subarray of AAA from index 111 to index jjj.
A function f(B)f(B)f(B) is defined on an array BBB as the number of subarrays of BBB which have sum less than KKK.

Let S=‚ąĎi=1N‚ąĎj=1Nf(Ai,j)S = \sum_{i = 1}^N \sum_{j = 1}^N f(A_{i, j})S=‚ąĎi=1N‚Äč‚ąĎj=1N‚Äčf(Ai,j‚Äč). Find the value of SSS modulo 1,000,000,0071,000,000,0071,000,000,007.

###Input:


The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two integers NNN and KKK.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.
###Output:
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äē the value of SSS modulo 1,000,000,0071,000,000,0071,000,000,007.

###Constraints


1‚Č§T‚Č§101 \le T \le 101‚Č§T‚Č§10
1‚Č§N‚Č§2‚ąó1051 \le N \le 2*10^51‚Č§N‚Č§2‚ąó105
‚ą£K‚ą£‚Č§1015|K| \le 10^{15}‚ą£K‚ą£‚Č§1015
‚ą£Ai‚ą£‚Č§109|A_i| \le 10^9‚ą£Ai‚Äč‚ą£‚Č§109 for each valid iii
###Sample Input:

`3
3 1
0 0 0
2 1
1 -1
1 1
-1
`###Sample Output:

`30
5
1
`###Explanation
In the second test case, the array is [1,‚ąí1][1, -1][1,‚ąí1] and K=1K = 1K=1.

f(A1,1)=0f(A_{1, 1})= 0f(A1,1‚Äč)=0

f(A1,2)=2f(A_{1, 2}) = 2f(A1,2‚Äč)=2, because both the subarrays [1,‚ąí1][1, -1][1,‚ąí1] and [‚ąí1][-1][‚ąí1] have sum <K< K<K.

f(A2,1)=2f(A_{2, 1}) = 2f(A2,1‚Äč)=2, because the array we are considering now is [‚ąí1,1][-1, 1][‚ąí1,1], and both the subarrays [1,‚ąí1][1, -1][1,‚ąí1] and [‚ąí1][-1][‚ąí1] have sum <K< K<K.

f(A2,2)=1f(A_{2, 2}) = 1f(A2,2‚Äč)=1

The sum of these is 5, which is the answer.
